title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: lehdr R package provides programmatic access Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Origin-Destination Employment Statistics (LODES) dataset. LODES forms backbone U.S. Census Bureau’s OnTheMap web application, maps commuting patterns fine geographic scales. OnTheMap useful exploratory tool, support systematic comparisons time across geographies. lehdr fills gap exposing LODES downloads tidy data frames providing built-functions common analyses. LODES contains three file types: OD (lodes_type = "od"): Origin-destination flows, giving number workers home-workplace Census block pair. RAC (lodes_type = "rac"): Residential area characteristics, giving job totals workers’ home blocks. WAC (lodes_type = "wac"): Workplace area characteristics, giving job totals workers’ job blocks.

title: Getting Started with lehdr
code: # From CRAN install.packages("lehdr")  # Development version from GitHub devtools::install_github("jamgreen/lehdr")  library(lehdr) library(dplyr)

what: Downloading a single state and year
title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: following example retrieves 2020 Oregon OD data aggregated Census tract level. primary jobs (job_type = "JT01") across worker segments (segment = "S000") included.
code: or_od <- grab_lodes(   state      = "or",   year       = 2020,   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "tract" )  head(or_od)

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: Pass character vectors state /numeric vectors year. function returns single bound tibble across combinations. state-year combinations available every LODES version. Consult LEHD Technical Document availability.
code: or_ri_od <- grab_lodes(   state      = c("or", "ri"),   year       = c(2019, 2020),   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "tract" )  head(or_ri_od)

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: agg_geo controls output geography. Valid values "block" (default), "bg" (block group), "tract", "county", "state". Aggregation truncates GEOID strings sums numeric columns.
code: md_rac_county <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "rac",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  head(md_rac_county)

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: Set geometry = TRUE retrieve Census geometries via tigris package attach result. lodes_type = "rac" "wac", return value sf object. lodes_type = "od", two geometry columns (h_geometry, w_geometry) added object remains tibble — use sf::st_as_sf() promote .
code: md_wac_sf <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county",   geometry   = TRUE )  plot(md_wac_sf["C000"])

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: grab_crosswalk() downloads LODES geographic crosswalk, maps Census blocks higher-level geographies. Useful need custom aggregations covered agg_geo.
code: vt_xwalk <- grab_crosswalk("vt")  # LODES7 vintage crosswalk (2010 Census blocks) vt_xwalk_7 <- grab_crosswalk("vt", version = "LODES7")

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: lehdr includes three functions common LODES analyses. examples use Maryland, rich varied labor market geography: large central city (Baltimore), dense inner suburbs, agricultural Eastern Shore counties, strong federal-sector employment Washington suburbs.

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: compute_commute_stats() summarizes commute flows OD dataset per-geography metrics: workers arriving (inflow), workers departing (outflow), internal flows, net flow, self-containment ratio. Self-containment share employed residents also work within geographic unit — value close 1 indicates largely self-sufficient labor market, values near 0 indicate heavy -commuting. Net flow identifies counties net job importers (positive) versus net exporters (negative), key indicator job/housing imbalance. map shows self-containment Census tract level Baltimore City, pattern reflects city’s mix dense employment centers residential neighborhoods limited local job access.
code: od_md <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "county" )  commute_md <- compute_commute_stats(od_md, agg_geo = "county")  # Most self-contained counties commute_md %>%   arrange(desc(self_containment)) %>%   select(county, workers_in, workers_out, net_flow, self_containment) %>%   head(10) # See data-raw/render_vignette_figures.R for the full figure code. # Requires: ggplot2, sf, tigris, scales

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: compute_lodes_change() computes absolute percentage change LODES variable two years. output can wide (one row per geography, columns variable’s change) long (one row per geography-variable combination, suitable ggplot2). map shows percent change total jobs (C000) across Maryland counties 2010 2019. diverging palette highlights counties gained jobs (blue) versus lost jobs (red) period.  Long format useful visualizing change across multiple variables :
code: wac_md_panel <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = c(2010, 2019),   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  change_md <- compute_lodes_change(   wac_md_panel,   geo_col      = "w_county",   base_year    = 2010,   compare_year = 2019,   variables    = c("C000", "CE01", "CE02", "CE03") )  # Counties with the largest absolute job growth change_md %>%   arrange(desc(C000_change)) %>%   select(w_county, C000_base, C000_compare, C000_change, C000_pct_change) %>%   head(10) # See data-raw/render_vignette_figures.R for the full figure code. # Requires: ggplot2, sf, tigris, scales change_long <- compute_lodes_change(   wac_md_panel,   geo_col   = "w_county",   output    = "long",   variables = c("CE01", "CE02", "CE03") )  # Suitable for use with ggplot2 facets or grouped geom_col() head(change_long)

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: compute_earnings_share() computes distribution jobs (resident workers) across three monthly earnings tiers defined LODES: WAC RAC files include columns; type controls geography prefix used auto-detection (w_ WAC, h_ RAC). Use output = "long" get format ready stacked bar charts: chart shows earnings tier composition Maryland counties 2019, sorted share low-wage jobs. Counties larger low-wage share tend rural tourism-dependent, suburban counties closer Washington, D.C. show higher shares well-paid jobs.  Combining compute_earnings_share() compute_lodes_change() supports longitudinal tracking wage polarization. Compute shares year separately, join difference:
code: wac_md <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  earn_shares <- compute_earnings_share(wac_md, type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county")  # Counties with the highest share of low-wage jobs earn_shares %>%   arrange(desc(share_low)) %>%   select(w_county, share_low, share_mid, share_high) %>%   head(10) earn_long <- compute_earnings_share(   wac_md,   type    = "wac",   geo_col = "w_county",   output  = "long" )  head(earn_long) # See data-raw/render_vignette_figures.R for the full figure code. # Requires: ggplot2, tigris, scales wac_panel <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = c(2010, 2019),   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  share_2010 <- compute_earnings_share(   dplyr::filter(wac_panel, year == 2010),   type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county" )  share_2019 <- compute_earnings_share(   dplyr::filter(wac_panel, year == 2019),   type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county" )

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: figures vignette produced ggplot2 Census geometries tigris package. general pattern : Download LODES data grab_lodes(). Compute derived variable one analytical functions . Fetch geometries tigris::tracts(), tigris::counties(), etc. Join GEOID column plot geom_sf(). following example shows simple flow-weighted job accessibility index Baltimore City tracts. home tract, metric sums total number workers flowing reachable work tracts — proxy labor market connectivity can computed entirely LODES OD data. n_dest captures breadth access (number distinct job destinations tract sends workers ), total_reachable flow-weighted measure aggregate connectivity. full accessibility analysis typically incorporates travel times distances (see packages r5r accessibility); LODES-measure useful starting point network data unavailable.
code: od_md <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "tract" )  accessibility <- od_md %>%   dplyr::filter(startsWith(h_tract, "24510")) %>%   dplyr::group_by(h_tract) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     n_dest          = dplyr::n_distinct(w_tract),     total_reachable = sum(S000, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups         = "drop"   )  head(accessibility) # See data-raw/render_vignette_figures.R for the full figure code. # Requires: ggplot2, sf, tigris, scales

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: default, downloaded files deleted reading. Setting use_cache = TRUE retains files user-level cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", "cache")), avoids re-downloading call grab_lodes() repeatedly parameters. can also set option globally session: cache filename includes LODES version (e.g., lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz), switching LODES versions serve stale data different vintage. Upgrading lehdr < 1.2.0? Earlier versions cached files without version prefix (e.g., md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz). files never matched new naming scheme can safely deleted. clear entire cache:
code: options(lehdr_use_cache = TRUE) cache_dir <- tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", "cache") list.files(cache_dir)          # inspect before deleting unlink(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with lehdr
text: use lehdr published work, please cite . Running citation("lehdr") return BibTeX entry. Chicago author-date style: Green, Jamaal, Liming Wang, Dillon Mahmoudi. 2025. “lehdr: Grab Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Flat Files.” R package version 1.2.0. https://github.com/jamgreen/lehdr/

what: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: lehdr (pronounced: lee dur like metric litre) R package allows users interface Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Origin-Destination Employment Statistics (LODES) dataset, returned tidy data frames. package designed download LODES flat files (origin-destination, workplace area characteristics, residential area characteristics), aggregate common Census geographies, now includes built-analytical functions commute flow statistics, longitudinal job change, earnings tier shares. Full documentation Getting Started vignette available lehdr pkgdown site. detailed walkthrough analytical functions maps, see Getting Started vignette.

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: can install released version lehdr CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("lehdr") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jamgreen/lehdr")

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: Load library optionally set lehdr_use_cache option save downloaded files reuse across sessions: grab_lodes() function downloads data specific state, year, LODES version. table type set lodes_type: origin-destination ("od"), residential area characteristics ("rac"), workplace area characteristics ("wac"). example, Oregon (state = "") 2020 (year = 2020) LODES version 8 (version = "LODES8", default), origin-destination (lodes_type = "od"), primary jobs (job_type = "JT01", default), worker segments (segment = "S000", default), aggregated Census tract level (agg_geo = "tract"): Pass character vectors state numeric vectors year retrieve multiple states years single call: years available state. full availability matrix, see LEHD Technical Document https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8. Set geometry = TRUE join Census geometries via tigris package. lodes_type = "rac" "wac", grab_lodes() returns sf data frame directly: lodes_type = "od", separate h_geometry w_geometry columns returned. can combined flow lines: optional version parameter selects LODES vintage. LODES8 (default) uses 2020 Census blocks. LODES7 uses 2010 Census blocks ends 2019. LODES5 uses 2000 Census blocks ends 2009.
code: library(lehdr)  options(lehdr_use_cache = TRUE) or_od <- grab_lodes(   state      = "or",   year       = 2020,   version    = "LODES8",   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "tract" )  head(or_od) or_ri_od <- grab_lodes(   state      = c("or", "ri"),   year       = c(2013, 2014),   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT01",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "tract" )  head(or_ri_od) ri_rac_geo <- grab_lodes(   state      = "ri",   year       = 2020,   lodes_type = "rac",   agg_geo    = "county",   geometry   = TRUE )  plot(ri_rac_geo["C000"]) or_od_geo <- grab_lodes(   state      = "or",   year       = 2020,   lodes_type = "od",   agg_geo    = "county",   geometry   = TRUE,   state_part = "main" )  h_to_w_geometry <- lapply(   seq(nrow(or_od_geo)),   function(i) {     sf::st_linestring(       c(         sf::st_centroid(or_od_geo[["h_geometry"]][[i]]),         sf::st_centroid(or_od_geo[["w_geometry"]][[i]])       )     )   } )  h_to_w_lines <- sf::st_as_sfc(h_to_w_geometry, crs = 4269)  or_od_lines <- sf::st_set_geometry(   or_od_geo[, c("w_county", "h_county", "h_geometry", "S000")],   h_to_w_lines )  multnomah_od_lines <- dplyr::filter(or_od_lines, w_county == "41051")  plot(multnomah_od_lines["S000"], reset = FALSE) plot(or_od_geo["h_geometry"], lwd = 0.25, add = TRUE)

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: lehdr includes three functions common LODES analyses.

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: compute_commute_stats() takes OD data frame returns per-geography inflow, outflow, internal flow, net flow, self-containment ratio. Self-containment share employed residents also work within geographic unit. map shows self-containment Census tract level Baltimore City, Maryland (FIPS 24510):
code: od_md <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "od",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   state_part = "main",   agg_geo    = "county" )  commute_md <- compute_commute_stats(od_md, agg_geo = "county")  commute_md |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(self_containment)) |>   dplyr::select(county, workers_in, workers_out, net_flow, self_containment) |>   head(10)

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: compute_lodes_change() computes absolute percentage change LODES variable two years. Output can wide (one row per geography) long (suitable ggplot2). map shows percent change total jobs across Maryland counties 2010 2019:
code: wac_md_panel <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = c(2010, 2019),   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  change_md <- compute_lodes_change(   wac_md_panel,   geo_col      = "w_county",   base_year    = 2010,   compare_year = 2019,   variables    = c("C000", "CE01", "CE02", "CE03") )  change_md |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(C000_change)) |>   dplyr::select(w_county, C000_base, C000_compare, C000_change, C000_pct_change) |>   head(10)

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: compute_earnings_share() computes distribution jobs across LODES earnings tiers: low ($1,250/month), mid ($1,251-$3,333/month), high ($3,333/month). Use output = "long" ggplot2-ready format.
code: wac_md <- grab_lodes(   state      = "md",   year       = 2019,   lodes_type = "wac",   job_type   = "JT00",   segment    = "S000",   agg_geo    = "county" )  earn_shares <- compute_earnings_share(wac_md, type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county")  earn_shares |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(share_low)) |>   dplyr::select(w_county, share_low, share_mid, share_high) |>   head(10)

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: figures produced ggplot2 tigris. general pattern : download grab_lodes(), compute derived variable, fetch geometries tigris::tracts() tigris::counties(), join GEOID, plot geom_sf(). See vignette("getting_started") full figure code. map shows flow-weighted job accessibility index Baltimore City tracts, computed directly LODES OD data:

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: figures can reproduced running source("data-raw/render_vignette_figures.R") locally. map shows self-containment Census tract level Baltimore City, Maryland — simple powerful use LODES OD data understand neighborhoods strong local job access versus heavy -commuting:  See vignette("getting_started") full figure code additional maps Maryland county job change, earnings tier composition, tract-level job accessibility.

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: Downloaded files deleted reading default. Set use_cache = TRUE grab_lodes() options(lehdr_use_cache = TRUE) globally retain files user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", "cache")). Cache filenames include LODES version (e.g., lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz), switching versions serve stale data different vintage. Upgrading lehdr < 1.2.0? Earlier versions cached files without version prefix (e.g., md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz). files never matched new naming scheme can safely deleted. clear entire cache:
code: cache_dir <- tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", "cache") list.files(cache_dir)          # inspect before deleting unlink(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE)

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: LODES dataset frequently used transportation economic development planners, regional economists, disaster managers, public servants need fine-grained understanding spatial distribution employment. data underpins regional travel demand models, workforce industrial policy analysis, , Census product, can joined Decennial American Community Survey data illuminate relationship population employment geography. LODES source detailed geographic information employment entire country, lehdr designed make accessible researchers analysts working regional development issues.

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: use lehdr published work, please cite : Green, Jamaal, Liming Wang, Dillon Mahmoudi. 2025. “lehdr: Grab Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Flat Files.” R package version 1.2.0. https://github.com/jamgreen/lehdr/
code: citation("lehdr")

title: lehdr: Access LODES Data in R
text: package developed Jamaal Green, University Pennsylvania; Dillon Mahmoudi, University Maryland Baltimore County; Liming Wang, Portland State University. package exist current format without inspiration Bob Rudis’s lodes package.

what: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
title: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
text: Derives three key commute flow metrics OD tibble: inflow, outflow, net flow, self-containment ratio. metrics widely used transportation planning economic geography characterize labor market catchment areas job/housing balance. Self-containment defined proportion workers live work within geographic unit, relative employed residents. value close 1 indicates highly self-contained labor market; values near 0 indicate heavy -commuting. Net flow defined inbound workers minus outbound workers (including internal flows ). unsigned flow balance indicator: positive values signal net job importers (workers arrive leave); negative values signal net exporters.

title: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
code: compute_commute_stats(od_df, agg_geo = "tract")

title: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
text: od_df data frame (tibble) LODES origin-destination data returned grab_lodes() lodes_type = "od". Must include columns h_{agg_geo} (home geography), w_{agg_geo} (work geography), S000 (total job count). data frame may aggregation level supported agg_geo. agg_geo geographic level OD data. Must match level od_df retrieved aggregated. One "block", "bg", "tract", "county", "state". Defaults "tract".

title: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
text: tibble one row per geography, containing: {agg_geo} geographic identifier. year Year data (present od_df). state State FIPS abbreviation (present od_df). workers_in Total workers arriving (working geography). workers_out Total workers departing (living , working elsewhere). workers_internal Workers whose home work geography (internal flows). net_flow Net worker flow: workers_in - workers_out. Positive values indicate net job importers. self_containment Share resident workers also work geography: workers_internal / workers_out_total, workers_out_total includes internal flows.

title: Compute commute flow statistics from LODES OD data — compute_commute_stats
code: # \donttest{   od <- grab_lodes(     state = "md", year = 2019,     lodes_type = "od", job_type = "JT00",     segment = "S000", state_part = "main",     agg_geo = "county"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/md/od/md_od_main_JT00_2019.csv.gz to lodes8_md_od_main_JT00_2019.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_md_od_main_JT00_2019.csv.gz #> lodes8_md_od_main_JT00_2019.csv.gz cleared from cache.   compute_commute_stats(od, agg_geo = "county") #> compute_commute_stats(): reducing 576 origin-destination pairs to one row per county. #> # A tibble: 24 × 8 #>    county  year state workers_in workers_out workers_internal net_flow #>    <chr>  <int> <chr>      <dbl>       <dbl>            <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 24001   2019 MD         20669        5676            16866    -1873 #>  2 24003   2019 MD        252820      130836           110416    11568 #>  3 24005   2019 MD        368877      223785           169441   -24349 #>  4 24009   2019 MD         20325       19774            12124   -11573 #>  5 24011   2019 MD          7256       11111             3713    -7568 #>  6 24013   2019 MD         49617       54659            26959   -32001 #>  7 24015   2019 MD         22114       15977            15076    -8939 #>  8 24017   2019 MD         32785       32125            16699   -16039 #>  9 24019   2019 MD         10550        9010             5421    -3881 #> 10 24021   2019 MD         88894       63841            50939   -25886 #> # ℹ 14 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: self_containment <dbl> # }

what: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
title: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
text: Computes share jobs (workers) three LODES monthly earnings tiers: Low (CE01) Earnings $1,250/month. Mid (CE02) Earnings $1,251-$3,333/month. High (CE03) Earnings $3,333/month. Earnings shares useful tracking wage polarization, identifying low-wage job concentration, examining earnings structure labor market shifted time, especially combined compute_lodes_change(). total denominator sum three tiers, ensuring shares sum 1 within rounding error.

title: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
code: compute_earnings_share(   lodes_df,   type = c("wac", "rac"),   geo_col = NULL,   output = c("wide", "long") )

title: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
text: lodes_df data frame (tibble) LODES RAC WAC data returned grab_lodes() segment = "S000" (total count segment, includes three earnings columns). Must contain columns CE01, CE02, CE03. WAC RAC files include earnings tier columns; type argument controls geography prefix used auto-detection. type One "wac" (workplace area characteristics, default) "rac" (residential area characteristics). Controls expected geography column prefix (w_ WAC, h_ RAC) geo_col = NULL. affect earnings columns used, file types share CE01/CE02/CE03 schema. geo_col name geography column group , e.g. "w_tract" "h_county". Defaults NULL, auto-detects first geography column lodes_df. output One "wide" (default) "long". Wide format appends three share columns. Long format returns one row per geography-tier combination.

title: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
text: tibble earnings tier counts shares. "wide" format, columns added share_low, share_mid, share_high. "long" format, columns tier, label, count, share.

title: Compute earnings tier shares from LODES RAC or WAC data — compute_earnings_share
code: # \donttest{   wac <- grab_lodes(     state = "md", year = 2019,     lodes_type = "wac", job_type = "JT00",     segment = "S000", agg_geo = "county"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/md/wac/md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz to lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz #> lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz cleared from cache.   compute_earnings_share(wac, type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county") #> # A tibble: 24 × 10 #>    w_county  year state count_low count_mid count_high total_earnings share_low #>    <chr>    <int> <chr>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>          <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 24001     2019 MD         6703     10657      10538          27898     0.240 #>  2 24003     2019 MD        56151     75065     139297         270513     0.208 #>  3 24005     2019 MD        85069    112452     200519         398040     0.214 #>  4 24009     2019 MD         5596      6699       9342          21637     0.259 #>  5 24011     2019 MD         1794      3460       3848           9102     0.197 #>  6 24013     2019 MD        14972     19219      23687          57878     0.259 #>  7 24015     2019 MD         6624      9991      14525          31140     0.213 #>  8 24017     2019 MD        10043     12431      14842          37316     0.269 #>  9 24019     2019 MD         2422      4597       4883          11902     0.203 #> 10 24021     2019 MD        22563     31364      50187         104114     0.217 #> # ℹ 14 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: share_mid <dbl>, share_high <dbl>    # Long format, suitable for ggplot2   compute_earnings_share(     wac, type = "wac", geo_col = "w_county", output = "long"   ) #> # what: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
title: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
text: Computes absolute percentage change LODES variables two years geographic unit. particularly useful tracking shifts employment, industrial composition, earnings structure time. Percentage change computed \((\text{compare} - \text{base}) / \text{base} \times 100\). Returns NA base_value zero missing.

title: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
code: compute_lodes_change(   lodes_df,   geo_col = NULL,   base_year = NULL,   compare_year = NULL,   variables = NULL,   output = c("wide", "long") )

title: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
text: lodes_df data frame (tibble) LODES data covering least two years, returned grab_lodes() vector years. Must contain year column one numeric columns difference. geo_col name geography column group , e.g. "w_tract", "h_county", "w_geocode". Defaults NULL, auto-detects first column ending _geocode, _tract, _county, _bg, _state. base_year reference year computing change. Defaults earliest year present lodes_df. compare_year target year computing change. Defaults latest year present lodes_df. variables Optional character vector numeric column names include output. Defaults numeric columns (excluding year). output One "wide" (default) "long". wide format, absolute percentage change columns appended variable. long format, variable row columns variable, base_value, compare_value, change, pct_change.

title: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
text: tibble change statistics. "wide" format, columns follow pattern {variable}_base, {variable}_compare, {variable}_change, {variable}_pct_change. "long" format, columns variable, base_value, compare_value, change, pct_change.

title: Compute longitudinal change in LODES data across years — compute_lodes_change
code: # \donttest{   wac_multi <- grab_lodes(     state = "md", year = c(2015, 2019),     lodes_type = "wac", job_type = "JT00",     segment = "S000", agg_geo = "county"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/md/wac/md_wac_S000_JT00_2015.csv.gz to lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2015.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2015.csv.gz #> lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2015.csv.gz cleared from cache. #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/md/wac/md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz to lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz #> lodes8_md_wac_S000_JT00_2019.csv.gz cleared from cache.   compute_lodes_change(wac_multi, geo_col = "w_county") #> # A tibble: 24 × 206 #>    w_county state C000_base CA01_base CA02_base CA03_base CE01_base CE02_base #>    <chr>    <chr>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 24001    MD        27705      6134     14795      6776      7596     11227 #>  2 24003    MD       257644     62430    136676     58538     59465     76402 #>  3 24005    MD       376473     89637    197685     89151     92004    117928 #>  4 24009    MD        21709      5542     11292      4875      5819      7084 #>  5 24011    MD         8502      1696      4502      2304      1780      3414 #>  6 24013    MD        57748     14436     28877     14435     16164     19685 #>  7 24015    MD        28963      7271     15186      6506      7061      9939 #>  8 24017    MD        37835     10428     19379      8028     11104     12475 #>  9 24019    MD        10810      2296      5476      3038      2513      4666 #> 10 24021    MD        95932     21874     52087     21971     22770     31070 #> # ℹ 14 more rows #> # ℹ 198 more variables: CE03_base <dbl>, CNS01_base <dbl>, CNS02_base <dbl>, #> #   CNS03_base <dbl>, CNS04_base <dbl>, CNS05_base <dbl>, CNS06_base <dbl>, #> #   CNS07_base <dbl>, CNS08_base <dbl>, CNS09_base <dbl>, CNS10_base <dbl>, #> # Long format   compute_lodes_change(     wac_multi,     geo_col  = "w_county",     output   = "long",     variables = c("C000", "CE01", "CE02", "CE03")   ) #> # A tibble: 96 × 8 #>    w_county variable base_year compare_year base_value compare_value change #>    <chr>    <chr>        <int>        <int>      <dbl>         <dbl>  <dbl> #>  1 24001    C000          2015         2019      27705         27898    193 #>  2 24003    C000          2015         2019     257644        270513  12869 #>  3 24005    C000          2015         2019     376473        398040  21567 #>  4 24009    C000          2015         2019      21709         21637    -72 #>  5 24011    C000          2015         2019       8502          9102    600 #>  6 24013    C000          2015         2019      57748         57878    130 #>  7 24015    C000          2015         2019      28963         31140   2177 #>  8 24017    C000          2015         2019      37835         37316   -519 #>  9 24019    C000          2015         2019      10810         11902   1092 #> 10 24021    C000          2015         2019      95932        104114   8182 #> # ℹ 86 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: pct_change <dbl> # }

what: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
title: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
text: Download LODES geographic crosswalk one states. crosswalk maps Census block GEOIDs higher-level geographies useful custom aggregations outside built-agg_geo argument grab_lodes().

title: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
code: grab_crosswalk(   state,   version = c("LODES8", "LODES7", "LODES5"),   download_dir = normalizePath(file.path(tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", which = "cache")),     mustWork = FALSE) )

title: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
text: state US state abbreviation lower case. Can vector state abbreviations, e.g. c("wy", "nd", "sd"). version LODES version whose crosswalk download. Must one "LODES8" (default), "LODES7", "LODES5". crosswalk maps Census blocks higher-level geographies vintage. download_dir Directory crosswalk file downloaded. Defaults user-level cache directory lehdr.

title: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
text: tibble containing geographic crosswalk Census block level, columns linking blocks block groups, tracts, counties, states.

title: Download and load LODES geographic crosswalk into a data frame (tibble) — grab_crosswalk
code: # \donttest{   # Download crosswalk for Vermont   vt_xwalk <- grab_crosswalk("vt") #> Downloading crosswalk https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/vt/vt_xwalk.csv.gz to lodes8_vt_xwalk.csv.gz #> Download complete for lodes8_vt_xwalk.csv.gz #> lodes8_vt_xwalk.csv.gz crosswalk cleared from cache.    # Download crosswalk for several small states   small_xwalk <- grab_crosswalk(c("wy", "nd", "sd")) #> Downloading crosswalk https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/wy/wy_xwalk.csv.gz to lodes8_wy_xwalk.csv.gz #> Download complete for lodes8_wy_xwalk.csv.gz #> Downloading crosswalk https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/nd/nd_xwalk.csv.gz to lodes8_nd_xwalk.csv.gz #> Download complete for lodes8_nd_xwalk.csv.gz #> Downloading crosswalk https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/sd/sd_xwalk.csv.gz to lodes8_sd_xwalk.csv.gz #> Download complete for lodes8_sd_xwalk.csv.gz #> lodes8_wy_xwalk.csv.gz crosswalk cleared from cache. #> lodes8_nd_xwalk.csv.gz crosswalk cleared from cache. #> lodes8_sd_xwalk.csv.gz crosswalk cleared from cache.    # Download a LODES7 crosswalk (2010 Census block vintage)   vt_xwalk_7 <- grab_crosswalk("vt", version = "LODES7") #> Downloading crosswalk https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES7/vt/vt_xwalk.csv.gz to lodes7_vt_xwalk.csv.gz #> Download complete for lodes7_vt_xwalk.csv.gz #> lodes7_vt_xwalk.csv.gz crosswalk cleared from cache. # }

what: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
title: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
text: Download LODES OD, RAC, WAC tables LEHD FTP server return tidy data frame.

title: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
code: grab_lodes(   state,   year,   version = c("LODES8", "LODES7", "LODES5"),   lodes_type = c("od", "rac", "wac"),   job_type = c("JT00", "JT01", "JT02", "JT03", "JT04", "JT05"),   segment = c("S000", "SA01", "SA02", "SA03", "SE01", "SE02", "SE03", "SI01", "SI02",     "SI03"),   agg_geo = c("block", "bg", "tract", "county", "state"),   state_part = c("", "main", "aux"),   download_dir = normalizePath(file.path(tools::R_user_dir("lehdr", which = "cache")),     mustWork = FALSE),   geometry = FALSE,   use_cache = getOption("lehdr_use_cache", FALSE),   ... )

title: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
text: state US state abbreviation lower case, character. Can vector states, like c("", "md", "tx") Oregon, Maryland, Texas. Two-letter FIPS abbreviations . year Year LODES data, numeric integer. Can vector years, like c(2014, 2020) 2014 2020. Must 2002 2023. version LODES version use. "LODES8" (default) enumerated 2020 Census blocks covers 2002-2022. "LODES7" enumerated 2010 Census blocks covers 2002-2019. "LODES5" enumerated 2000 Census blocks covers 2002-2009. lodes_type LODES table type. Values can default origin-destination ("od"), residential area characteristics ("rac"), workplace area characteristics ("wac"). OD files give home workplace census block worker flow. RAC files give job totals worker home blocks; WAC files give job totals worker job blocks. job_type Job type segment: "JT00" jobs (default), "JT01" Primary Jobs, "JT02" Private Jobs, "JT03" Private Primary jobs, "JT04" Federal jobs, "JT05" Federal Primary jobs. segment Workforce segment. "S000" total jobs (default); "SA01" workers aged 29 younger; "SA02" workers aged 30-54; "SA03" workers 55 older; "SE01" earnings $1,250/month less; "SE02" earnings $1,251-$3,333/month; "SE03" earnings $3,333/month; "SI01" Goods Producing industries; "SI02" Trade, Transportation, & Utilities industries; "SI03" Services. agg_geo Aggregate geography Census Block (default). Values can "bg" (block group), "tract", "county", "state". string "block group" also accepted alias "bg". state_part Part state file; required lodes_type = "od". "main" includes workers whose home workplace -state. "aux" includes workers live --state work state interest. Defaults "main" warning lodes_type = "od" state_part explicitly supplied. download_dir Directory LODES file downloaded. Defaults user-level cache directory lehdr. geometry TRUE, use tigris package download attach spatial geometries U.S. Census Bureau specified year level specified agg_geo. Returns sf object lodes_type "rac" "wac". use_cache Boolean. TRUE, reuse previously downloaded files rather re-downloading. Defaults FALSE. can also set lehdr_use_cache global option TRUE make caching default session. ... Additional arguments passed tigris functions geometry = TRUE.

title: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
text: tibble LODES data aggregated block, block group, tract, county, state level. geometry = TRUE lodes_type "rac" "wac", returns sf object.

title: Download and load LODES data into a data frame (tibble) — grab_lodes
code: # \donttest{   # Download 2014 block-level OD data for Vermont   blk_od <- grab_lodes(     state = "vt", year = 2014,     lodes_type = "od", job_type = "JT01",     segment = "SA01", state_part = "main"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/vt/od/vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz to lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz #> lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz cleared from cache.    # Download 2014 OD data for Vermont aggregated to tract level   trt_od <- grab_lodes(     state = "vt", year = 2014,     lodes_type = "od", job_type = "JT01",     segment = "SA01", state_part = "main",     agg_geo = "tract"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/vt/od/vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz to lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz #> lodes8_vt_od_main_JT01_2014.csv.gz cleared from cache.    # Download 2020 RAC data for Vermont aggregated to tract level   trt_rac <- grab_lodes(     state = "vt", year = 2020,     lodes_type = "rac", job_type = "JT01",     segment = "SA01", agg_geo = "tract"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/vt/rac/vt_rac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz to lodes8_vt_rac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_vt_rac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz #> lodes8_vt_rac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz cleared from cache.    # Download 2020 WAC data for Vermont aggregated to tract level   trt_wac <- grab_lodes(     state = "vt", year = 2020,     lodes_type = "wac", job_type = "JT01",     segment = "SA01", agg_geo = "tract"   ) #> Downloading https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/vt/wac/vt_wac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz to lodes8_vt_wac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz... #> Download complete: lodes8_vt_wac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz #> lodes8_vt_wac_SA01_JT01_2020.csv.gz cleared from cache. # }

what: lehdr: Grab Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Flat Files — lehdr-package
title: lehdr: Grab Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Flat Files — lehdr-package
text: Designed query Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) workplace/residential association origin-destination flat files optionally aggregate Census block-level data block group, tract, county, state. Also provides analytical functions commute flow statistics, longitudinal change, earnings tier distributions. Data comes LODES FTP server https://lehd.ces.census.gov/data/lodes/LODES8/.

title: lehdr: Grab Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) Flat Files — lehdr-package
text: Maintainer: Jamaal Green jamaal.green@gmail.com Authors: Jamaal Green jamaal.green@gmail.com Liming Wang lmwang@pdx.edu Dillon Mahmoudi dillonm@umbc.edu contributors: Matthew Rogers matthew.rogers09@gmail.com [contributor] Kyle Walker kyle@walker-data.com [contributor] Eli Pousson eli.pousson@gmail.com (ORCID) [contributor]
